target.title: feat: add AlpaCore bridge, ACA deploy flow, and harvester UI
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: local AlpaCore work was split across ad hoc MCP/editor setup, manual device approval fallbacks, and one-off Azure/container steps; non-loopback Control UI binds could also miss persisted allowed origins.
- Why it matters: that made local MCP usage, device approval, ACA deployment, and harvester monitoring slower and more failure-prone.
- What changed: added a local AlpaCore MCP bridge for VS Code/Cursor, seeded and merged Control UI allowed origins at startup, added a local loopback fallback for `openclaw devices` list/approve, added azd/Bicep plus a from-package ACA deploy helper, and added the harvester service plus control UI plus scripts/docs/Postman assets.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no broad plugin/runtime architecture rewrite, no provider behavior overhaul, and no live Azure rollout is included in this PR.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #29385
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: local editor/gateway/Azure flows depended on manual setup and assumed direct RPC scope access; startup did not persist extra Control UI origins for non-loopback/container binds, and local pairing approval had no safe fallback path when pairing-required or missing-scope errors occurred.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no startup merge for `OPENCLAW_CONTROL_UI_ALLOWED_ORIGINS`, no loopback-only fallback for local pairing approval/listing, and no scripted/tested `azd deploy openclaw --from-package` path.
- Contributing context (if known): this branch also adds a new harvester service/control UI, so the surrounding local/dev infra had to be wired end to end.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/cli/devices-cli.test.ts`, `src/gateway/startup-control-ui-origins.test.ts`, `test/scripts/azd-deploy-openclaw-from-package.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: loopback pairing fallback for `devices list/approve`, Control UI allowed-origin seeding/merge on startup, and Windows-aware `azd deploy ... --from-package` helper behavior.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: these cases live at CLI/startup/script seams and can be validated without a full live gateway or Azure deployment.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): `startup-control-ui-origins` and deploy helper tests are included here; devices CLI fallback coverage is expanded here.
- If no new test is added, why not: harvester UI/server additions were only target-validated in this pass; no full UI automation was added yet.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- VS Code/Cursor can call a local MCP bridge that proxies Arni/health/readiness into AlpaCore on port 8080.
- Control UI allowed origins are seeded and persisted for non-loopback binds and can be extended via `OPENCLAW_CONTROL_UI_ALLOWED_ORIGINS`.
- `openclaw devices` list/approve can fall back to local pairing files for local loopback setups when remote scope access blocks direct RPC.
- New Azure deploy helper builds and pushes the OpenClaw image and calls `azd deploy openclaw --from-package`.
- New harvester backend plus control UI provides live logs, target queue/status, worker stats, auth helpers, and Postman examples.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes`)
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes`)
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: the branch adds a local MCP bridge, harvester endpoints, and Azure deployment plumbing, but the sensitive paths stay bounded: the CLI fallback only activates for local loopback without explicit `--url`, extra Control UI origins are explicit and logged, request timeouts are enforced for the bridge, and Azure secrets still flow through env/Key Vault rather than being committed.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Windows
- Runtime/container: Node 24.15.0, local Git working tree
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): local AlpaCore MCP bridge, gateway Control UI, Azure `azd` packaging helper
- Relevant config (redacted): `ALPACORE_URL`, `ALPACORE_TIMEOUT_MS`, `OPENCLAW_CONTROL_UI_ALLOWED_ORIGINS`, `HARVESTER_PUBLIC_URL`

### Steps

1. Run `git diff --cached --check` during the commit pass.
2. Run `node --check harvester-server.mjs`.
3. Run `node --check control-ui/vite.config.js`.
4. Parse the changed JSON files with a Node one-liner.
5. Push the rewritten branch history to `origin/feat/mcp-bridge`.

### Expected

- Targeted syntax/format/JSON validation passes and the branch pushes cleanly to `origin`.

### Actual

- Those targeted checks passed.
- Full `pnpm`/Vitest/Azure deployment validation was not run in this pass.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: The Azure Harvester deployment slice of this PR is live and serving the new Revolut canonical-note status surface. The live Container App now exposes `/api/revolut/status` with the canonical signer-refresh note and is up enough to accept live client traffic.
- Real environment tested: Azure Container Apps live Harvester service `azhargtunnpdilgmg2` in resource group `rrg-dev` on subscription `12561c86-6070-4d32-998a-71834ff653d1`, endpoint `https://azhargtunnpdilgmg2.calmsmoke-1bc10097.westeurope.azurecontainerapps.io`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. `curl.exe -sS "https://azhargtunnpdilgmg2.calmsmoke-1bc10097.westeurope.azurecontainerapps.io/api/revolut/status"`
  2. `curl.exe -sS "https://azhargtunnpdilgmg2.calmsmoke-1bc10097.westeurope.azurecontainerapps.io/api/health"`
  3. `az containerapp logs show --name azhargtunnpdilgmg2 --resource-group rrg-dev --subscription 12561c86-6070-4d32-998a-71834ff653d1 --tail 20`
- Evidence after fix:

```text
Live /api/revolut/status summary
{
  "configured": false,
  "signerConfigured": false,
  "refreshTokenPresent": false,
  "clientAssertionType": "urn:ietf:params:oauth:client-assertion-type:jwt-bearer",
  "canonicalNoteId": "revolut-signer-refresh-v1",
  "canonicalNoteOverview": "Harvester uses a signer-based client assertion flow for Revolut token refresh.",
  "canonicalRefreshFields": "grant_type, refresh_token, client_assertion_type, client_assertion",
  "canonicalRules": "client_assertion_type is fixed and always sent | client_id is only sent when configured as a compatibility fallback | refresh_token stays in runtime/env scope | JWT stays runtime-only and is never persisted | private key material must stay outside Postman and outside Harvester state",
  "lastError": "REVOLUT signer base URL, signer token, and refresh token must be configured"
}

Live /api/health summary
{
  "status": "ok",
  "dbConfigured": true,
  "dbConnected": true,
  "persistenceReady": true,
  "stripeSecretSource": "keyvault",
  "revolutConfigured": false,
  "revolutSignerConfigured": false,
  "revolutCanonicalNoteId": "revolut-signer-refresh-v1"
}

Azure Container Apps log excerpt
[
  {
    "TimeStamp": "2026-05-10T12:30:37.4063",
    "Log": "Successfully Connected to container: 'harvester' [Revision: 'azhargtunnpdilgmg2--azd-1778339287', Replica: 'azhargtunnpdilgmg2--azd-1778339287-59689cb8cc-5qtn7']"
  },
  {
    "TimeStamp": "2026-05-10T12:06:23.4158589+00:00",
    "Log": "New WebSocket client connected"
  },
  {
    "TimeStamp": "2026-05-10T12:06:23.6947901+00:00",
    "Log": "New WebSocket client connected"
  }
]
```
- Observed result after fix: The live Harvester endpoint returns the canonical Revolut signer-refresh note on Azure, `/api/health` reports `status: ok` with database connectivity ready, and Azure runtime logs show the `harvester` container connected on the active revision and serving live WebSocket traffic.
- What was not tested: I did not verify a live Revolut refresh with signer secrets populated, I did not correct the live Stripe secret in this proof pass, and this proof section does not claim end-to-end runtime proof for the non-Harvester slices of the branch.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: reviewed branch diff vs `main`, validated staged diff cleanliness before commit, checked Node syntax for new entrypoints, parsed changed JSON files, rewrote unpublished commit authors for GitHub privacy compliance, pushed the branch to `origin/feat/mcp-bridge`, and verified live Harvester behavior on Azure using endpoint output and Container Apps logs.
- Edge cases checked: loopback-only fallback guard in devices CLI, origin merge de-duplication, Windows command resolution in the Azure helper.
- What you did **not** verify: full `pnpm check`, full `pnpm test`, live Stripe secret remediation, live Revolut refresh with signer secrets populated.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`Yes`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: the PR spans multiple surfaces, so review breadth is wider than ideal.
  - Mitigation: the main behavior changes are additive and grouped into editor bridge, gateway fallback/origins, Azure deploy helper, and harvester UI/service slices with targeted tests on the non-UI logic.
- Risk: local fallback could hide remote permission problems if it ran too broadly.
  - Mitigation: it only activates for local loopback flows and is disabled when `--url` is explicitly provided.
- Risk: misconfigured Control UI origins could overexpose the UI.
  - Mitigation: origin seeding is explicit, deduped, persisted, and logged; extra origins only come from deliberate config/env input.
target.author: finnur-fk
